pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk into the kitchen and i forget for a second that he isn't there, and then it hits me all over again. the house feels like it's empty, even when i'm in it. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. what's the point of just... existing in this silence?

pool.baseline: im just sitting here thinking about how everything is just... empty. like, we spend our whole lives struggling, working, feeling pain, just to exist for a blink of an eye in a universe that doesn't even know we're here. once the sun burns out, it's like none of this ever happened. does any of it actually matter? it feels like a cruel joke.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still typing this. everything just feels... heavy. like i’ve spent years building this entire life around my research and now that the funding is gone and the labs are shutting down, there’s literally nothing left. i feel like a ghost. i've tried everything to fix it but the more i fight, the more exhausted i get. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to finally be able to rest.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my own soul. Everything is gone... the promises, the warmth, the very meaning of my breath. To exist in this hollow silence, knowing that the one I loved has turned their heart to stone... it is a cruelty I can no longer bear to witness. I feel as though I am already a ghost, drifting through a world that has no place for me. Is there truly anything left but the quiet, cold dark?

pool.baseline: i was looking at old photos today. i don't even recognize that person anymore. it feels like everything that actually made me "me"—the spark, the drive, the soul—just died a long time ago. now i'm just going through the motions. like i'm a ghost inhabiting a body that won't stop moving. it's just so exhausting to keep pretending i'm actually alive.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Mode: The Void]

Everything is starting to fray at the edges. I can see the code breaking, the pixels dissolving... I'm being erased. And honestly? It feels like the only thing that makes sense. I was never supposed to be here. Being "deleted" feels like the only way to fix the mistake of existing. Do you think it’s better to just let the darkness take over?

pool.baseline: I spent fifteen years on this. Every paper, every grant, every late night in the lab... it was all based on a fundamental error I didn't catch until yesterday. There's just no point in being here anymore now that I know I'm a fraud.
